title: cardamine californica subsp. cuneata
intro: Cardamine californica subsp. cuneata, also known as Cuneate Western Bittercress, is a perennial plant species native to the Pacific coast of North America. It is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, alleviating symptoms of colds and flu, and supporting the immune system. Cardamine californica subsp. cuneata can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
